behaviors.description: A plugin whose manifest is missing a required field is rejected before any code from that plugin is executed.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is between manifest validation and module loading. A unit test of only the validator can’t prove loader never imports/executes plugin code. Use real loader wiring with filesystem plugins so a bad manifest plugin with import side effects can reveal accidental execution.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temp plugins directory with one plugin whose `manifest.json` omits a required field and whose entry module writes a sentinel file (or increments a shared marker) at import time. Run the real loader against the directory. Assert: (1) loader reports manifest validation error, (2) sentinel side effect did not occur, (3) plugin `onLoad` was never called.

behaviors.description: When plugin A declares a dependency on plugin B, plugin B's `onLoad` hook is called before plugin A's.
behaviors.justification: This is dependency graph resolution + lifecycle orchestration across multiple plugins. Unit tests on topological sort alone may miss hook invocation ordering bugs in loader integration.
behaviors.test_description: Create plugins A and B in a temp directory where A depends on B. Each `onLoad` appends its name to a shared call log. Run the loader and assert call order is exactly `["B","A"]`. Also assert both loaded successfully.

behaviors.description: When two plugins each declare a dependency on the other, the loader reports the cycle and loads neither.
behaviors.justification: Cycle detection correctness depends on interaction between manifest parsing, graph build, and load execution gating. A unit test of cycle algorithm alone can miss bugs where loader still executes one plugin despite detection.
behaviors.test_description: Create plugins A and B where A depends on B and B depends on A; both entry modules have import-time side effects and `onLoad` logging. Run loader. Assert: (1) cycle error/report includes A↔B, (2) no import side effects fired, (3) no `onLoad` executed for either plugin.

behaviors.description: A plugin's `onLoad` hook is only invoked after the plugin's entry module has been imported and executed.
behaviors.justification: Ordering bug appears at module-loader/lifecycle boundary. Mocking import can hide real sequencing errors; need real module execution side effect before `onLoad` assertion.
behaviors.test_description: Create a plugin whose entry module sets `globalThis.pluginInitFlag = true` during import, and whose `onLoad` throws if the flag is not set. Run real loader with real import path resolution. Assert loader succeeds and recorded events show import side effect happened before `onLoad` entry.

behaviors.description: When a plugin throws an error during its `onLoad` hook, the loader continues initializing the remaining plugins that do not depend on it.
behaviors.justification: This is failure isolation across plugin graph execution, requiring real interaction of dependency checks, error handling, and continuation policy. Unit tests of try/catch logic alone won’t catch scheduler/graph gating regressions.
behaviors.test_description: Create plugins: `X` (throws in `onLoad`), `Y` (independent), `Z` (depends on X), `W` (depends on Y). Run loader. Assert: (1) X failure is reported, (2) Y and W `onLoad` were still called, (3) Z was not loaded because it depends on failed X, (4) process did not abort global initialization.
